title: Malty Cozy Welsh Cheese-pull Fried
description: Crispy katsuobushi-crusted Welsh rarebit fritters that melt to a gooey cheese core, perfect for snuggling up on a cold winter's night.

ingredients.name: katsuobushi
ingredients.notes: dried, shaved into flakes, toasted in a 350°F oven for 5 minutes, then ground into a fine powder

instructions: Combine flour, cornstarch, katsuobushi powder, and salt in a bowl; add a pinch of black pepper.
In a separate bowl, mix egg, heavy cream, and Welsh rarebit sauce.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until a thick batter forms.
Dip each piece of cheese into the batter, coating it evenly, then roll in the toasted katsuobushi flakes to coat.
Heat 1/2 cup of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F.
Carefully add the battered cheese to the hot oil and fry until golden brown on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the fritters from the oil with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve immediately, sprinkled with additional katsuobushi flakes if desired.
Repeat with the remaining cheese pieces and batter.
Serve with a side of butter or additional Welsh rarebit sauce, if desired.
